openai.evidence: runs/20260505-132352-openai-product-manager-api-infrastructure/04-after-submit.png shows yellow Ashby banner: 'we have set up limits for applications across roles. Candidates may not apply more than 5 times in any 180 day span'. Backfilled manually because run predates ashby.py classifier refactor.

sierra.evidence: runs/20260505-133400-sierra-product-manager-agent-studio rejected 16 minutes after Sierra Agent SDK SUCCESS. User observed RED 'flagged as possible spam' banner. Classifier emitted kind=rejected because validation_visible:true masked the spam banner phrase. Manually backfilled to enforce 72h tenant cooldown.

modal.evidence: runs/20260505-192937-modal-customer-engineer — User clicked submit in --prep mode (real human click, only 3 fields: name/email/resume). Ashby flagged the application as possible spam. Confirms that --prep alone does NOT defeat Ashby's anti-bot detection; browser fingerprint / fill timing / IP-rep are also signals.
